extraction.one_line_summary: ASGREEN attacked solo at 4.8km after Deceuninck-Quick-Step deployed all seven riders in pre-Taaienberg team time trial, isolating VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quick-Step executed coordinated seven-rider team time trial before Taaienberg at 66km, creating numerical superiority for later attacks.
ASGREEN attacked at 4.8km despite being theoretically spent, exploiting three teammates marking VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT simultaneously.
VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT's Paterberg move forced premature effort while Quick-Step numbers controlled, inverting normal isolation dynamics.
Wind-assisted race fragmentation favored Quick-Step's depth over individual marquee riders' attacking strength in finale.

rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Won despite appearing spent, revealing Quick-Step's willingness to sacrifice domestiques' legs early for late numerical advantage.
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: Isolated despite attacking Paterberg with VAN AERT; Quick-Step's three-rider marking negated solo class in finale.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Marked out of contention despite making selection; unable to counter when theoretically weaker ASGREEN attacked.
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: Bridged to lead group mid-race, demonstrating Quick-Step's depth extended beyond traditional climbers on hellingen.
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: neutral: Made early breakaway showing sprinters can survive initial selections when peloton controls tempo.
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: neutral: Former winner in breakaway suggests Quick-Step willing to sacrifice experience for numbers behind.
